_schema_note: 1.1 = test_results split into backend/frontend sub-objects (established W10); everything else mirrors the W9 1.0 shape.
wave: Wave D3 (self-healing lanes, W17 part 1: EM-177 lane failover with recovery probes, EM-168 cap-pressure governor, EM-171 background cache-key normalization re-measure, EM-172 mid-round-death scheduler fix + energy-band hysteresis)

resolution.summary: QE gate for Wave D3 on build/wave-d3-lane-health (B1 d2ce65d, B2 3d90859, B3 1ad6e90; backend/config byte-identical from 1ad6e90 to HEAD — the later EM-178/179/181 commits are the user's own web/ work outside this gate). Adversarial mandate executed: (1) EM-177 detour reality — the shipped test_lane_failover.py §F already asserts on recording-ADAPTER CALL LOGS (beta.calls == [(777, 0.3)], alpha.calls == []), not return values, so the substitute adapter object provably receives the chat() call at the substitute's max_tokens/temperature and the home adapter receives the probe; re-run green. (2) The 2026-06-11 starvation shape (all lanes sick except one) had no in-repo proof — now permanent in backend/tests/test_wave_d3_qe.py: 3 agents on 3 different sick home lanes converge all 18 real calls on the single healthy lane at ITS budget (640, 0.4), each home adapter receives exactly its 2 probe calls at the HOME budget (512, 0.8), mock and non-home sick lanes receive ZERO calls, spans carry requested_profile + detoured/probe, one degraded edge per home lane, and a second test drives clean probes until a home lane recovers (degraded→recovered exactly once). (3) EM-168 demotion reality — beyond the shipped _tier_due predicate test, a new QE test drives the REAL next_agent() rotation: a demoted protagonist drops from 9 scheduled turns to exactly 3 over rounds 4-12 (supporting cadence, rounds 6/9/12) while the control protagonist keeps 9, and both the alert-path restore and the zero-clock usage-window-probe path put it back to every-round scheduling. (4) EM-171 — the 91.7% (11 hits / 1 miss, 1 adapter call for 12 turns) reproduces deterministically across 3 runs (exact-equality assertion stats == {hits:11, misses:1, entries:1}); harness honesty checked: non-mock stub adapter (mock is uncacheable by design), cache-on matches production (app.py builds Router with default cache_enabled=True), the salience-holding proposed rule provably never enters the prompt (only ACTIVE rules render; the vote menu is governance-gated), and the protagonist control misses 4/4. (5) EM-172 regression honesty — the fix reverted in a /tmp copy of backend/ (repo tree never modified; import shadowing verified to the copy): exactly the 3 reproduction tests FAIL pre-fix ('c' returned where 'b' was due — the skip), 8 non-dependent tests still pass; with the fix all 11 pass. (6) Full suite 550 passed (546 wave baseline + 4 new QE tests). (7) Assertion-quality scan of the three new test files found no tautologies (disabled-path tests pin exact pre-D3 payload key SETS; event tests assert exact phase sequences); free-scale law upheld (effective_profile re-routes calls that would happen anyway, probes REPLACE would-be detours, reflex turns resolve before the failover seam so they never tick the probe cadence — code-verified); config/snapshot round-trips complete for both new blocks (yaml → dataclass → runs.config_json → restore, EMBEDDED_WORLD_YAML mirror, shipped yaml pinned; demoted_from/cap_demotions snapshot round-trip incl. unknown-value rejection; EM-177 failover state is deliberately in-memory-only per contract). Four MINOR findings, the sharpest being that run.py (the CLI/mock entry) builds Router(cfg.profiles) WITHOUT threading world.lane_failover and never wires the lane-event/usage-alert sinks or the usage-window probe — the yaml knob is honored only by the API server. Zero blockers; gate GREEN.

contract_conformance.notes: Verified against contracts/wave-d3.md clause-by-clause, by reading the shipped source and by execution. B1: sickness predicate (threshold 3, mock never, provider_error excluded), healthiest-candidate detour with load-spread tie-break and stable order, all-sick ⇒ home (never mock, never a lost turn), per-call detours with assigned profile untouched, probe-every-4 cadence ticked only by would-be-detours, runtime resolves effective_profile ONCE per turn AFTER the cadence gate (reflex turns can't tick the cadence), effective lane's budgets used, outcome attribution to the effective lane, additive llm_call keys with the pre-D3 key set pinned byte-exact on healthy/disabled paths, lane_detour edges exactly twice per cycle, GET /api/lanes shape, config block per EM-155 conventions (loader dataclass, runs.config_json round-trip, embedded mirror, shipped yaml). B2: one-tier demotion with background floor, demoted_from recorded, once-per-lane-alert-day idempotence, manual override wins and is never re-demoted same-day, rollover-first restoration through both the alert path and the injected zero-clock window probe, snapshot/fork round-trip with pre-D3 key-set preservation, enabled:false byte-identical (snapshot-equality proven), EM-177 composition (demoted agent on a sick lane still detours — adapter-call-record proof). B3: tick line dropped / memory de-ticked / rosters+menus sorted for BACKGROUND only with protagonist controls and the pre-diet fixture guard intact; realized hit rate re-measured and recorded (91.7%); _turn_index fix regression-proven; hysteresis with genuine-crossing preservation. DOCKED ONE POINT for two honest dents: (a) the world.lane_failover knob is threaded only by the API entry — backend/petridish/run.py:65 builds Router(cfg.profiles) bare, so enabled:false/custom thresholds in world.yaml are silently ignored on CLI runs (defaults coincide with shipped yaml, so default behavior is identical); (b) the contracted 'enabled: false ⇒ byte-identical pre-D3 behavior' is therefore proven for the router+API path but is unenforceable from config on the CLI path.

test_coverage.notes: 87 new wave tests (39 failover + 28 governor + 20 B3) + 4 permanent QE tests (backend/tests/test_wave_d3_qe.py), all green in a 550-test suite. Quality is high: the failover §F tests assert on adapter CALL RECORDS at specific budgets (the green-gate≠real-fix rule was already engineered in); disabled-path tests pin exact payload key SETS, not just absence of new keys; the governor suite covers idempotence, manual-override interaction, snapshot round-trip incl. hostile demoted_from values, and the cross-feature composition with EM-177; test_scheduler_skip.py's reproduction test PROVABLY fails pre-fix (verified by execution against a reverted /tmp copy: 3 fail, 8 pass). QE adds the two missing scenario shapes: the all-sick-but-one incident convergence (with probe/home-budget/zero-mock call-record proofs and probe-driven recovery) and real-rotation demotion cadence counting (9→3→9 turns across demote/restore, both restore paths). Honest gaps, none load-bearing: (1) no test pins run.py's Router wiring — which is exactly where finding 1 shipped; (2) EM-171's realized hit rate is pinned only in the single-agent harness — no multi-agent integration measurement exists in-repo, so a future regression of the city-scale rate (where co-location SET churn still breaks keys) would go unmeasured, same gap shape the D2 report recorded; (3) the lane_detour 'recovered' feed event's agent attribution (whichever agent makes the first healthy call) is untested — cosmetic.

security.notes: New surface is one read-only endpoint (GET /api/lanes — no parameters, serves router.lane_health() introspection over in-memory state; no secrets, no prompts, no message bodies) plus system feed events generated from internal sinks (lane_detour, cap_pressure — text built from repo-side profile names and agent names, never from model output). No new request shapes accept user input beyond the pre-existing tier endpoint. All sinks are defensively wrapped (never raise into chat()/turn paths). Free-scale law upheld and code-verified: failover re-routes calls that would already happen, probes substitute for detours (never additive), the governor only REMOVES scheduled LLM turns, and EM-171 only changes prompt bytes. Tests use fake keys via monkeypatched env vars and recording adapters pointed at localhost:9 — no network, no real keys, MockProvider/fakes only per the contract's verification law. Failover/demotion state cannot be injected from snapshots into unsafe values (demoted_from validated against CADENCE_TIERS on restore, fail-safe to None).

regression_risk.notes: Everything is additive and config-gated, and both gates carry byte-identical-disabled proofs (exact key-set pins for B1, snapshot-equality for B2); full suite 550/550 with the 546 wave baseline intact. The real risk surface is that D3 touches three hot paths: Router.chat() routing (per-call detours — bounded by the effective_profile seam, guarded getattr keeps duck-typed test routers unchanged), World.next_agent() (the _turn_index decrement — 11 dedicated tests incl. round-boundary/tail-death edges plus the executed pre-fix failure proof), and background prompt assembly (live BEHAVIOR change: background agents now see no tick line and de-ticked memories — deliberate, but worth watching live since model outputs may shift; protagonist/supporting pinned byte-identical by the pre-diet fixture). One pre-existing test assertion was changed (test_wave_d2_prompt_diet.py 'Tick: day 2' → no Tick: line) — reviewed, strictly reflects the superseding EM-171 behavior, and the EM-162 energy-bucket assertions it sits with are untouched. Watch items: live detour behavior on a genuinely degraded proxy night (the unit windows are fed by note_parse_outcome; the 12s-budget timeout is the only demerit source, so a lane that errors fast rather than slow never sickens — by design per EM-173, but it means the failover does nothing for the deepseek-hung-outright failure mode unless those surface as timeouts), and the run.py wiring gap (finding 1) if anyone demos failover from the CLI.

frontend.result_line: not run — Wave D3 is contractually backend-only (no agent touched web/); the web/ commits on this branch after 1ad6e90 (EM-178/179/181, header link) are the user's own live work explicitly outside the wave gate, and backend/+config/ are byte-identical from 1ad6e90 to HEAD (verified by git diff --stat)

test_results.qe_probes: Verified BY EXECUTION: (1) full suite 550 passed (and 546 passed pre-QE-file, matching the wave claim); (2) EM-172 reversion experiment — backend/ copied to /tmp/qe-d3-revert, import resolution to the copy verified (petridish.engine.world.__file__ = /private/tmp/qe-d3-revert/...), the _turn_index decrement removed, pytest tests/test_scheduler_skip.py: 3 FAILED (test_mid_round_death_before_pointer_skips_no_one with AssertionError 'c' == 'b', test_every_living_due_agent_acts_exactly_once_despite_two_deaths, test_midround_death_with_tiered_due_set) / 8 passed; real tree: 11/11 pass; repo source files never modified (an earlier in-tree attempt was abandoned before any test ran and world.py restored from HEAD); /tmp copy deleted, git status clean apart from the user's README/START-HERE edits; (3) EM-171 determinism — test_wave_d3_cache.py run 3 consecutive times, 9 passed each, and the hit-rate test pins stats == {hits: 11, misses: 1, entries: 1} by exact equality so 91.7% (11/12) is reproduced on every run; (4) test_lane_failover.py re-run green (39 passed); (5) the 4 new QE tests (incident convergence, probe recovery, demotion cadence, probe-path restore) passed against the shipped code. Verified BY READING: router.py effective_profile/lane_sick/_pick_detour_candidate logic (incl. that all-sick calls don't tick the probe cadence and streak state clears on clear_cache); runtime.py run_turn resolves the failover seam ONCE per turn after the cadence gate with effective-lane budgets and outcome attribution; world.py governor invariants (all cap_demotions records provably share one window because every write path restores stale windows first); EM-171 harness honesty (production Router defaults cache-on — app.py:123 passes no cache kwargs; world.cache CacheParams is parsed but never threaded anywhere, pre-existing W7 gap with coinciding defaults; the salience-holding proposed rule never renders — only ACTIVE rules enter the prompt and the vote menu is governance-place-gated); the changed test_wave_d2_prompt_diet.py assertion (strictly reflects EM-171 superseding the day-floored tick; orchestrator-reviewed); assertion-quality scan of all three new test files (no tautologies found); free-scale scan (no new standing LLM call anywhere in the diff); app.py sink/probe wiring and the reassign endpoint keeping agent.profile in sync with the router (governor scoping depends on it).

issues.title: run.py (CLI entry) ignores world.lane_failover config and never wires the D3 sinks — failover knobs and the cap governor are API-server-only
issues.detail: backend/petridish/api/app.py:123-126 threads cfg.world.lane_failover into Router and wires set_lane_event_sink / set_usage_alert_sink / set_usage_window_probe, but backend/petridish/run.py:65 still builds Router(cfg.profiles) bare. Consequences on a CLI run (python -m petridish.run): (a) the lane_failover yaml block is silently ignored — enabled:false or custom sick_threshold/probe_every have no effect (hardcoded defaults apply, which happen to equal the shipped yaml values, so DEFAULT behavior is identical); (b) lane_detour edge events are dropped (no sink); (c) the EM-168 governor never engages (usage alerts have no sink, the world has no window probe). The contract's files-owned lists never included run.py, so this is a wiring gap rather than a contract violation — but the contracted 'enabled:false ⇒ byte-identical pre-D3' guarantee is unreachable from config on this entry point. Cheap fix: mirror app.py's three wiring lines in run.py; a pin test in test_wave_d3_qe.py would then keep the two entry points from drifting again.

issues.title: EM-171's 91.7% is real and deterministic but measured in a single-agent idealized harness — do not bank it for the 25-agent city
issues.detail: The re-measure (test_wave_d3_cache.py::test_em171_realized_cache_hit_rate_background) drives real run_turn turns through a real Router with a non-mock recording adapter and reproduces 11 hits / 1 miss on every run — the mechanism payoff is genuine, and each of the three D2-diagnosed key-breakers (day drift, tick-stamped memory, roster/menu ordering churn) has its own unit pin with protagonist controls. But the D2 report's 0% was measured at 25 agents, and this harness is one solo background agent whose memory window holds identical 'Solo idles.' lines. The normalization sorts rosters (ORDER churn) but cannot help when reflex move-home changes WHO is co-located (SET churn) or when a background agent's own actions vary — both of which the D2 P2 measurement identified at city scale. Realized city-scale hit rate will land somewhere in (0%, 91.7%); capacity planning should keep pricing background LLM-consulting turns as real calls until a multi-agent integration measurement exists. Recorded so the 91.7% headline number is not quoted as a city-scale property.

issues.title: Pre-existing (W7, not D3): world.cache config is parsed into CacheParams but never threaded into the Router by any entry point
issues.detail: config/world.yaml carries a world.cache block (enabled, max_entries) and loader.py parses it (CacheParams, loader.py:259-264, :882), but neither app.py:123 nor run.py:65 passes it to Router(cache_enabled=, cache_max=) — the router always runs its own defaults (enabled, 512), which happen to equal CacheParams' defaults, so behavior matches the yaml AS SHIPPED. Surfaced during the EM-171 harness-honesty check (it confirms production cache is unconditionally ON, validating the harness assumption — the finding cuts in the wave's favor). A user setting cache.enabled:false or a custom max_entries would be silently ignored. Pre-dates D3 (W7/EM-068 era); filed for plan-intake, not against this wave.

issues.title: lane_detour 'recovered' event attributes agent_id to whichever agent happens to make the first healthy call
issues.detail: router.py effective_profile: the recovery edge fires inside the healthy branch on the NEXT call for that home lane after the window ages clean, and stamps that caller's agent_id — which can be a different agent than the one whose detours/probes drove the recovery (any agent assigned to the lane, or in principle one that was reassigned onto it). The degraded edge has the same whoever-triggered-it semantics. Feed-text cosmetic only ('✓ lane recovered — X is back home' may name the wrong agent on multi-agent lanes); payload home/substitute/phase are always correct, per-turn truth rides the llm_call payload, and the exactly-two-edges-per-cycle invariant holds (QE-tested). Fix only if the feed copy ever confuses watchers.

gate_decision.reason: All Wave D3 hard gates are green and the wave's load-bearing claims survive adversarial verification by execution, not by trust: the full suite is 550/550 ('550 passed, 1 warning in 27.04s'; 546 wave baseline intact + 4 QE tests); EM-177's detour is REAL — the substitute adapter object receives the chat() call at the substitute's budget while the home adapter receives exactly the probe calls (shipped §F tests assert on call records, and the new QE incident-shape test proves the 2026-06-11 starvation scenario end-to-end: 3 agents on 3 sick lanes converge 18/18 real calls on the single healthy lane, 2 probes per home lane at home budgets, zero calls to mock or to sick non-home lanes, recovery via clean probes with exactly one degraded+recovered edge pair); EM-168's demotion changes actual scheduling, not a label (real next_agent() rotation: 9→3 turns per 9 rounds, restored to 9 via both the alert path and the zero-clock probe path); EM-171's 91.7% reproduces deterministically across 3 runs through a production-shaped cache configuration with an honest harness (with a recorded caveat that the number is harness-scale, not city-scale); and EM-172's reproduction test provably FAILS without the fix (3 failures in a reverted /tmp copy, 'c' where 'b' was due) and passes with it. Both new mechanisms are config-gated with proven byte-identical disabled paths, snapshot/fork round-trips are complete, the free-scale law holds (zero new standing LLM calls — probes substitute, never add), and the one changed pre-existing assertion is the orchestrator-reviewed strictly-stronger EM-171 supersession. Gate rules: zero CRITICAL, zero MAJOR, contract 4 >= 3, security 5 >= 3. GREEN — W17 part 2 may start; the four MINORs (run.py failover/sink wiring gap, city-scale hit-rate unknown, pre-existing world.cache threading gap, recovered-edge agent attribution) go to plan-intake.
